title: The Fibre Revolution
subtitle: Nationwide fibre deployment transforms Singapore's digital landscape
overview: The 2010s marked a fundamental transformation in Singapore's internet infrastructure. The government-mandated Next Generation National Broadband Network (NGNBN) brought fibre-to-the-home (FTTH) to almost every residential unit in Singapore. By 2012, Singapore achieved near-complete nationwide fibre coverage — one of the first countries in the world to do so. This decade saw speeds jump from single-digit megabits to gigabit connections, fundamentally changing what was possible online and making Singapore a global leader in connectivity.

timeline.description: OpenNet began deploying fibre-to-the-home connections across Singapore. Early adopters saw speeds of 100-200 Mbps.
Singapore reached 95% residential fibre coverage. ISPs began competing aggressively on price rather than availability.
Multiple ISPs launched 1Gbps residential plans at competitive prices. Singapore ranked in global top 5 for internet speed.
Low latency enabled local esports scene to flourish. Major tournaments held in Singapore with professional teams.
Singtel and M1 offered 10Gbps residential fibre for enthusiasts and prosumers, pushing boundaries even further.

gamingDescription: The fibre revolution transformed gaming in Singapore. Home connections became fast enough for competitive play, streaming became viable, and local esports scenes emerged. Games like Dota 2, League of Legends, and CS:GO could be played at professional levels from home.
gamingHighlights: Home internet became viable for competitive gaming
Popular games: Dota 2, League of Legends, CS:GO, Overwatch
Streaming and content creation became possible with upload speeds
Low ping (<20ms) enabled participation in regional tournaments
Cybercafés evolved into gaming lounges and esports venues
challenges: Expensive fibre installation costs for older buildings
Some HDB estates faced delays in fibre deployment
Price wars between ISPs led to frequent plan changes
Upgrading home networking equipment to handle gigabit speeds
